key_drivers: Iran war triggering major oil supply disruption (Hormuz risk) - CL, GC, 6J strongly bullish
Weak NFP (-92K jobs, unemployment 4.4%) driving rate cut expectations and real yield decline
VIX spiking to 29.48 (+24%) signals risk-off: bearish equities, bullish gold/yen/bonds
DXY weakening (-0.21%) on dollar weakness post-NFP, supporting commodities and EUR/JPY
Gold ETF inflows 9th consecutive month ($5.3bn in Feb) - structural gold demand intact

stale_sources: FRED T10Y2Y (using CNBC yield data for directional inference)
data_quality.confidence_notes: Pre-FOMC (March 17-18) reduces confidence by 1 point. Iran war geopolitical premium adds uncertainty to CL and GC.
